metadata.summary: Code Editor-style reprs for Earth Engine data in a Jupyter notebook.

documentation:
# eerepr

[![Earth Engine Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Earth%20Engine%20API-Python-green)](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/tutorials/community/intro-to-python-api)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/eerepr.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/eerepr)
[![conda-forge link](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/eerepr)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/eerepr)
[![nbviewer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jupyter/design/master/logos/Badges/nbviewer_badge.svg)](https://nbviewer.org/github/aazuspan/eerepr/blob/main/docs/notebooks/demo.ipynb)
[![Binder](https://mybinder.org/badge_logo.svg)](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/aazuspan/eerepr/HEAD?labpath=docs%2Fnotebooks%2Fdemo.ipynb)
[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/aazuspan/eerepr/blob/main/docs/notebooks/demo.ipynb)

Add interactive Code Editor-style HTML reprs to Earth Engine objects in a Jupyter environment.

![eerepr demo expanding the metadata for an image collection](assets/eerepr.gif)

## Setup

Install from PyPI:

```bash
$ pip install eerepr
```

Install from Conda-Forge:

```bash
$ conda install -c conda-forge eerepr
```

## Usage

```python
import eerepr
```

Importing `eerepr` in a Jupyter notebook adds an HTML repr method to all Earth Engine objects. When you print them, you'll see an interactive HTML repr instead of a boring old string repr. Simple as that!

> **Note**
> Just like in the Code Editor, printing huge collections can be slow and may hit memory limits.

## Caching

`eerepr` uses caching to improve performance. Server data will only be requested once for each unique Earth Engine object, and all subsequent requests will be retrieved from the cache until the Jupyter session is restarted.

When you import `eerepr`, it is automatically initialized with an unlimited cache size. You can manually set the number of unique objects to cache using `eerepr.initialize(max_cache_size=n)`. A value of `None` sets an unlimited cache while a value of `0` disables caching.

functions.name: eerepr.repr._load_js
documentation:
Note: JS is only needed because the CSS `:has()` selector isn't well supported yet, preventing
a pure CSS solution to the collapsible lists.

functions.name: eerepr.repr._attach_html_repr
documentation: Add a HTML repr method to an EE class. Only overwrite the method if it was set by this function.

functions.name: eerepr.repr._is_nondeterministic
documentation:
Check if an object returns nondeterministic results which would break caching.

Currently, this only tests for the case of `ee.List.shuffle(seed=False)`.

functions.name: eerepr.repr._repr_html_
documentation: Generate an HTML representation of an EE object.

functions.name: eerepr.repr._ee_repr
documentation: Wrapper around _repr_html_ to prevent cache hits on nondeterministic objects.

functions.name: eerepr.repr.initialize
documentation:
Attach HTML repr methods to EE objects and set the cache size.

Re-running this function will reset the cache.

Parameters
----------
max_cache_size : int, optional
    The maximum number of EE objects to cache. If None, the cache size is unlimited. Set to 0
    to disable caching.

functions.name: eerepr.html.convert_to_html
documentation:
Converts a Python object (not list or dict) to an HTML <li> element.

Parameters
----------
obj : Any
    The object to convert to HTML.
key : str, optional
    The key to prepend to the object value, in the case of a dictionary value or list element.

functions.name: eerepr.html.list_to_html
documentation: Convert a Python list to an HTML <li> element.

functions.name: eerepr.html.dict_to_html
documentation: Convert a Python dictionary to an HTML <li> element.

functions.name: eerepr.html._sort_dict
documentation:
Sort the properties of an Earth Engine object.

This follows the Code Editor standard where priority keys are sorted first and the rest are
returned in alphabetical order.

functions.name: eerepr.html._make_collapsible_li
documentation: Package a header and children into a collapsible list element

functions.name: eerepr.html._build_multipoint_label
documentation: This also works for LineString and LinearRing.

functions.name: eerepr.html._build_object_label
documentation: Build a label for a generic JS object.

functions.name: eerepr.html._build_typed_label
documentation: Build a label for an object with an unrecognized type.

functions.name: eerepr.html._build_label
documentation:
Take an info dictionary from Earth Engine and return a header label.

These labels attempt to be consistent with outputs from the Code Editor.
